title: Refactor RepoContext and startup wiring for unresolved startup and one-time root binding
summary: Refactor startup and repo-context handling so the MCP server can boot even when process CWD is '/' and then bind a validated project root exactly once for the session, preserving the 0.4.3 safety guarantee against resolving artifacts under '/.agent'.
acceptance_criteria: RepoContext can represent an unresolved startup state when process CWD resolves to '/'.
Normal non-'/' startup still binds the project root immediately for backward compatibility.
RepoContext exposes explicit one-time binding semantics for workspace initialization, including same-root no-op behavior and different-root rebind rejection.
Artifact path helpers and repo-root access continue to reject unsafe usage that would otherwise resolve to '/.agent'.

steps.description: Refactor RepoContext so startup may begin without a validated repo root while preserving explicit validation for any bound root.
Introduce one-time root-binding behavior and invariants for unresolved -> resolved transition, same-root rebind no-op, and different-root rebind failure.
Update main.py startup wiring so non-'/' CWD still binds immediately, but '/' starts in unresolved mode without crashing the server.
Ensure root accessors and artifact path construction fail clearly when root is unresolved or unsafe.

notes: This ticket establishes the runtime foundation for workspace_initialize(cwd) but does not itself expose the new tool contract.
Keep the change narrow: unresolved startup is allowed, but filesystem-backed behavior must still require a validated non-root project directory.
Preserve backward compatibility for ordinary single-project launches that already start from the project directory.
